nodes.speaker: Tev
lines: You've been flying far. Good. Far is what the ship's for.
I have a favor, and it's a strange one. Friends of mine live under the ice on the cold twin. We write letters. Paper letters — they don't... they don't do radio.

responses.buttonText: How many of us have you taken in, Tev?

nodes.speaker: Tev
lines: They'll tell you themselves. In their way.
It's not mine to write in someone else's hand.

nodes.speaker: Tev
lines: Researchers, once. Neighbors, always. Eleven years of letters, and every one of mine signed the same way.
One of them has been asking about you, you know. And I never told them a thing — which is how I know they'll like you.

nodes.speaker: Tev
lines: Mm. The one with the smallest handwriting.
Go and see. Some introductions shouldn't be made by a third party.

nodes.speaker: Tev
lines: Thank you. Now listen, because this part matters more than the letter.
When you're inside: don't speak. Not a word, not a whisper, no matter what you see.
Smile a lot. They like manners.

nodes.speaker: Tev
lines: The first one taught me the routine. The water, the fish, the fire. I only wrote it down.
Don't tell me how it ends this time. I find I do better work when I don't know.

responses.buttonText: Can I see what the first one wrote?

nodes.speaker: Tev
lines: (He takes a tin from the shelf, and from the tin, a folded paper worn soft as cloth.)
You've been reading my copy since your first morning. This is the hand it came from.
Bring it back before dark. It's the only thing in this house I'd chase you for.
